metadata.title: EU vs FDA Regulatory Divergences — Comprehensive Mapping
metadata.description: Maps specific differences between EU pharmaceutical legislation and US FDA requirements. Each divergence identifies the EU legal basis, the corresponding FDA framework, the nature of the difference, and practical compliance implications.

divergences.eu_requirement: EU has 27 national competent authorities plus EMA. Four authorization pathways: centralised (EMA), mutual recognition, decentralised, and national.
divergences.fda_requirement: Single federal agency (FDA) with one approval pathway (NDA/BLA). No state-level drug approval authority.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU requires strategic choice of authorization pathway. A product may need national translations, local legal representatives, and engagement with multiple authorities. FDA path is simpler but covers only one jurisdiction.

divergences.eu_requirement: Biotechnology products, ATMPs, orphan drugs, and products with new active substances for specified diseases MUST use centralised procedure.
divergences.fda_requirement: No mandatory pathway distinction by product type. All drugs/biologics go through FDA regardless of therapeutic area.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU applicants for biotech/ATMP/orphan products have no choice — must file centrally. This determines fees, timelines, and assessment body.

divergences.eu_requirement: Products not in mandatory centralised scope may use decentralised procedure (multi-state simultaneous) or mutual recognition (post-initial authorization). Reference Member State leads assessment; concerned Member States may only disagree on grounds of 'potential serious risk to public health.'
divergences.fda_requirement: No equivalent. FDA approval covers entire US territory automatically.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU applicants must manage multi-country regulatory strategy, translations, national transposition differences, and potential referral/arbitration procedures.

divergences.eu_requirement: MA may be granted for unmet medical needs (serious/life-threatening diseases) prior to comprehensive clinical data. Valid for 1 year, renewable, with annual reassessment of specific obligations. Risk-benefit must be favourable.
divergences.fda_requirement: Accelerated Approval allows approval based on surrogate endpoints, but grant is not explicitly time-limited to 1 year. Post-marketing confirmatory trials required. FDA can withdraw if trials fail.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU conditional MA creates annual renewal pressure with strict specific obligations. FDA accelerated approval has confirmatory study requirement but not fixed annual renewal.

divergences.eu_requirement: MA under exceptional circumstances when comprehensive data cannot be provided for objective, verifiable reasons. Linked to annual reassessment.
divergences.fda_requirement: No direct equivalent. Closest is Subpart H/I approval or Emergency Use Authorization.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has a distinct long-term authorization pathway for products where full data may never be obtainable, separate from emergency mechanisms.

02_data_exclusivity_and_IP.title: Data Exclusivity, Market Protection, and IP Incentives

divergences.eu_requirement: 8 years data exclusivity + 2 years marketing protection (8+2=10 total). Extendable to 11 years for significant new indication. Generic can file at year 8 but not market until year 10.
divergences.fda_requirement: NCE: 5 years data exclusivity; generic filing permitted at year 4 with Paragraph IV certification. New clinical investigation: 3 years additional exclusivity. Biologics: 12 years (4-year filing gate).
divergences.compliance_implication: EU provides longer small-molecule data exclusivity (8 vs 5 years) but shorter biologic protection (10 vs 12 years). The 8+2+1 EU structure means generics can file at year 8 and market at year 10 (or 11).

divergences.eu_requirement: 10 years of market exclusivity. Reduced to 6 years if prevalence criteria no longer met at year 5 or if product sufficiently profitable. Derogated if similar product clinically superior.
divergences.fda_requirement: 7 years of market exclusivity from date of approval. No reduction mechanism based on profitability. Exclusivity can be overcome by showing clinical superiority.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU provides longer orphan exclusivity (10 vs 7 years) but with clawback risk. EU definition uses 5/10,000 prevalence threshold vs FDA's 200,000-patient threshold.

divergences.eu_requirement: Completed PIP: 6-month SPC extension. Orphan products: 2 additional years market exclusivity (total 12 years). PUMA: 10 years data/marketing protection.
divergences.fda_requirement: Pediatric exclusivity: 6-month extension of any existing exclusivity/patent protection. Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) and Pediatric Research Equity Act (PREA).
divergences.compliance_implication: EU SPC extension applies only if SPC exists; FDA pediatric exclusivity extends any existing exclusivity. EU PUMA pathway has no US equivalent. EU paediatric obligation is triggered earlier (PIP required at PK study completion) vs US PREA at NDA/BLA stage.

divergences.eu_requirement: Where active substance in well-established medicinal use for at least 10 years within the Community with recognised efficacy and acceptable safety, test and trial results may be replaced by scientific literature.
divergences.fda_requirement: No direct equivalent. Closest is OTC monograph system or literature-based NDA (505(b)(2)), but these do not provide identical 10-year use substitution.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU offers a pure literature-based pathway for long-established substances. US 505(b)(2) allows reference to published literature but typically requires at least bridging studies.

divergences.eu_requirement: Single submission through EU portal for multi-country trials. Reporting Member State leads coordinated assessment with defined three-phase timelines (26+12+7 days). Tacit authorisation if Member State fails to respond within timelines.
divergences.fda_requirement: Single IND submission to FDA. 30-day review period; clinical hold if concerns. No multi-country coordination mechanism within US.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU provides a coordinated multi-state approval mechanism with strict timelines and tacit approval. FDA IND process is simpler but covers only the US. EU requires parallel ethics committee integration.

divergences.eu_requirement: Ethics committee review integrated into the regulatory assessment timeline (Part II). Each Member State assesses ethical aspects within same 45-day timeline. Ethics committee may cover aspects in both Part I and Part II.
divergences.fda_requirement: IRB review is a parallel, separate process from IND review. FDA does not integrate IRB timelines into its review. IRB approval required before trial starts but not as part of the IND submission.
divergences.compliance_implication: In the EU, regulatory and ethical review timelines are legally interlinked. A negative ethics opinion can prevent trial authorization. In the US, IRB and FDA processes run independently.

divergences.eu_requirement: Distinct category: trials using authorised products within or supported by evidence outside marketing authorisation terms, with minimal additional risk. Subject to less stringent monitoring, master file, and IMP traceability rules.
divergences.fda_requirement: No formal low-intervention category. All trials require IND unless exempt (21 CFR 312.2). Exempt trials must meet specific criteria but are not labeled as a separate regulatory category.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU provides a lighter regulatory pathway for pragmatic trials comparing approved treatments. US sponsors may use IND exemptions but without the same formal framework.

divergences.topic: Mandatory results disclosure and layperson summary
divergences.eu_requirement: Results summary shall be submitted to EU database within 1 year of trial end (6 months for paediatric). A layperson-understandable summary is mandatory. All trials must be registered in EU database before starting.
divergences.fda_requirement: ClinicalTrials.gov registration and results reporting required for applicable clinical trials within 1 year of primary completion date. No mandatory layperson summary.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU mandates layperson summary not required by FDA. EU database is the single portal for all trial communications. Both systems require timely results posting.

divergences.eu_requirement: Clinical trial master file shall be archived for at least 25 years after end of trial.
divergences.fda_requirement: Records retained for 2 years after approval of NDA/ANDA or investigation discontinuation. No 25-year mandate.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's 25-year retention is dramatically longer than FDA's 2-year post-approval minimum. Requires long-term archival infrastructure.

divergences.eu_requirement: Member States shall ensure compensation systems (insurance/guarantee/similar) are in place. Sponsor and investigator shall use the system appropriate to the Member State.
divergences.fda_requirement: No federal mandate for clinical trial insurance. Institutional policies and contractual arrangements govern. Tort liability applies.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU sponsors must arrange insurance/indemnification per each Member State's requirements. Costs vary significantly by country and trial risk profile.

divergences.eu_requirement: No gene therapy clinical trials may be carried out which result in modifications to the subject's germ line genetic identity.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA does not explicitly prohibit germ line editing trials by statute, but has maintained appropriations rider (since 2015) prohibiting FDA from reviewing applications involving intentional creation/modification of human embryos to include heritable genetic modifications.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU prohibition is permanent statute. US restriction is annual appropriations-dependent and technically narrower (embryo-specific vs any germ line modification).

divergences.eu_requirement: Fatal/life-threatening SUSARs: 7 days. All other SUSARs: 15 days. Reports to centralised Eudravigilance database.
divergences.fda_requirement: IND safety reports for unexpected fatal/life-threatening events: 7 calendar days. Other serious unexpected: 15 calendar days. Reports to FDA.
divergences.compliance_implication: Timelines are similar but reporting destination and format differ. EU uses structured Eudravigilance electronic submission; FDA uses MedWatch/CIOMS forms.

divergences.eu_requirement: At least one layperson shall participate in the assessment of each clinical trial application.
divergences.fda_requirement: No layperson participation required in IND review. IRBs require at least one member whose primary concerns are non-scientific.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU builds patient/public input directly into the regulatory assessment. US patient input is primarily through IRBs and advisory committee public hearings.

divergences.eu_requirement: Legally mandated individual with specified university qualifications (4-year course in pharmacy/medicine/chemistry/biology) and minimum 2 years practical experience. QP must personally certify each production batch in a register. Register retained 5 years.
divergences.fda_requirement: No QP equivalent. cGMP responsibilities are organizational (quality unit/quality control unit). No statutory requirement for a named individual with specified university qualifications to certify each batch.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU manufacturers must employ at least one QP per site. QP has personal legal liability for batch release. US firms operating in EU must either hire QPs or contract with EU entities that have them.

divergences.eu_requirement: Active substances imported into EU shall be accompanied by written confirmation from exporting country's competent authority confirming: GMP standards at least equivalent to EU; regular inspections conducted; non-compliance promptly communicated.
divergences.fda_requirement: No requirement for written government confirmation from exporting country. FDA relies on its own inspections of foreign API manufacturers and Drug Master Files (DMFs).
divergences.compliance_implication: EU requires government-to-government GMP certification for API imports. This can create supply chain bottlenecks for APIs from countries without MRA agreements with the EU.

divergences.eu_requirement: Within 90 days of inspection, competent authority shall issue GMP certificate if compliant. Certificates entered in publicly accessible Union database (EudraGMDP).
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA issues Establishment Inspection Reports (EIRs) and may issue warning letters. No formal GMP certificate system. Inspection outcomes tracked internally.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU GMP certificate is a positive attestation required for supply chain qualification. FDA's system is enforcement-focused (warning letters for non-compliance rather than certificates for compliance).

divergences.eu_requirement: Product specification file required containing all information necessary for written instructions on processing, packaging, QC, testing, and batch release of investigational medicinal products.
divergences.fda_requirement: No formal 'product specification file' concept for investigational drugs. IND contains chemistry, manufacturing, and controls (CMC) information. Manufacturing information evolves over development.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU requires a consolidated reference document (PSF) at the manufacturing site. FDA's CMC requirements are part of the IND application but not structured as a site-level reference file.

divergences.eu_requirement: Importers, manufacturers, and distributors of active substances shall register with competent authority at least 60 days prior to commencement. Entered in Union database.
divergences.fda_requirement: Drug establishment registration required (annual, within 5 days of beginning operations). FDA does not have a separate 60-day pre-commencement notification.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's 60-day advance notice requirement means API operations must be planned ahead. FDA registration is concurrent with or shortly after beginning operations.

divergences.eu_requirement: MAH shall have permanently and continuously a Qualified Person Responsible for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) who shall reside and operate in the Union.
divergences.fda_requirement: No equivalent named statutory individual. Regulations require adequate number of qualified employees for FDA contact. Typically involves VP of Drug Safety or Medical Officer.
divergences.compliance_implication: Non-EU companies must designate a QPPV based in the EU with personal legal responsibility for the pharmacovigilance system.

divergences.eu_requirement: MAH shall maintain a PSMF describing the pharmacovigilance system. Location declared to authority. Must be submitted within 7 days upon request.
divergences.fda_requirement: No equivalent PSMF requirement. FDA may inspect pharmacovigilance procedures but does not mandate a specific master file document.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU-specific document that must be maintained, updated, and made available on short notice. Requires dedicated pharmacovigilance documentation resources.

divergences.eu_requirement: Mandatory PSURs submitted electronically to EMA repository with defined frequency schedule (6 months initially, then annual, then every 3 years). Single assessment procedure with PRAC rapporteur, 60-day assessment, 30-day comment period. EU reference dates harmonise submission across Member States.
divergences.fda_requirement: Periodic Adverse Drug Experience Reports (PADERs) required quarterly for first 3 years, then annually. Less structured assessment process. No committee-based assessment comparable to PRAC.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU PSUR system is more elaborate with harmonised reference dates, work-sharing between Member States, PRAC assessment, and potential regulatory consequences. Significant ongoing compliance burden.

divergences.eu_requirement: Centralised EU-wide database for all suspected adverse reactions. MAH must submit electronically. Healthcare professionals and public have appropriate access to aggregated data.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS) is the US database. MedWatch reporting. FAERS data publicly accessible but less structured public access framework.
divergences.compliance_implication: Different reporting formats, different databases, different electronic submission requirements. Companies operating in both jurisdictions need dual reporting systems.

divergences.eu_requirement: Non-serious suspected adverse reactions within the Union SHALL be reported to Eudravigilance within 90 days.
divergences.fda_requirement: Non-serious adverse experiences need not be individually reported; included in periodic reports.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU requires individual case reporting even for non-serious events. Significantly higher volume of case processing compared to FDA requirements.

divergences.eu_requirement: Multi-state rapid safety procedure triggered by safety concerns. PRAC recommendation within 60 days. Member States may unilaterally suspend MA pending decision, notifying others next working day. Commission decision binding on all Member States.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA acts unilaterally (market withdrawal, labelling changes, REMS). No multi-authority coordination procedure. FDA can immediately suspend approval.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's system involves multi-stakeholder consultation and binding Commission decisions. Can result in EU-wide suspension. FDA process is faster for individual products but covers only US.

divergences.eu_requirement: Products on additional monitoring list (new actives, biologicals post-2011, conditional MAs) must include inverted black triangle symbol and statement 'This medicinal product is subject to additional monitoring' in SmPC and leaflet.
divergences.fda_requirement: No equivalent symbol or labelling requirement. FDA may require REMS or Boxed Warning for specific safety concerns.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU requires visible on-pack symbol encouraging ADR reporting for qualifying products. No labelling equivalent in US.

divergences.eu_requirement: RMP mandatory for all new MA applications (centralised and national). Must describe risk management system, safety specification, pharmacovigilance plan, risk minimisation measures. Submitted with MA application and updated throughout product lifecycle.
divergences.fda_requirement: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies (REMS) required only when FDA determines necessary to ensure benefits outweigh risks. Not mandatory for all products.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU mandates RMP for every new product. FDA REMS is exception-based. EU RMP is a comprehensive living document; REMS is typically more focused on specific risk mitigation actions.

divergences.eu_requirement: Product name shall be expressed in Braille format on packaging. Leaflet available in formats for blind/partially-sighted on request.
divergences.fda_requirement: No requirement for Braille on pharmaceutical packaging.
divergences.compliance_implication: All EU-marketed products need Braille-embossed packaging. Affects packaging design, procurement, and costs.

divergences.eu_requirement: Mandatory unique identifier and anti-tampering device on prescription products. EU-wide repositories system for verification. Costs borne by manufacturers. Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/161 specifies technical details.
divergences.fda_requirement: Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) requires serialization (product identifier on each package). Different technical implementation than EU (no anti-tampering device mandate, different verification system).
divergences.compliance_implication: Both require serialization but with different technical specifications, databases, and verification workflows. Dual compliance requires separate packaging lines or configurations.

divergences.eu_requirement: Package leaflet shall reflect consultations with target patient groups (readability testing mandatory).
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA encourages but does not mandate patient readability testing for prescription drug labelling. OTC Drug Facts labelling has specific format requirements but no patient testing mandate.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU applicants must conduct and document user testing of leaflets, adding time and cost to the labelling development process.

divergences.eu_requirement: Labelling and leaflet shall appear in official language(s) of each Member State where marketed. May be printed in several languages.
divergences.fda_requirement: English labelling required. No multi-language mandate.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU products marketed in multiple Member States need multi-language packaging, driving multiple SKUs or fold-out leaflets. Significant packaging/supply chain complexity.

divergences.eu_requirement: Structured SmPC with 12 defined sections is a legally binding document forming part of the MA decision. SmPC content legally constrains what MAH may claim.
divergences.fda_requirement: US Prescribing Information (USPI/PI) follows Physician Labeling Rule format with different sections and structure. Also legally binding but with different content requirements.
divergences.compliance_implication: SmPC and USPI are not interchangeable. Different section numbering, content requirements, and regulatory status require separate documents.

divergences.eu_requirement: Marketing authorisation application shall include evaluation of potential environmental risks posed by the medicinal product. Specific arrangements to limit environmental impact shall be envisaged on a case-by-case basis.
divergences.fda_requirement: Environmental assessment (EA) or categorical exclusion required per NEPA for NDA/BLA submissions. However, most applications qualify for categorical exclusion.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU ERA is substantively evaluated for all applications. FDA categorical exclusions mean most applications bypass environmental assessment entirely.

07_distribution_and_supply_chain.title: Distribution, Supply Chain, and Online Pharmacy

divergences.eu_requirement: MAH and distributors shall ensure appropriate and continued supply to pharmacies so patients' needs are covered.
divergences.fda_requirement: No binding supply continuity obligation. FDA Drug Shortage Prevention Act requires advance notice of supply disruptions for certain drugs, but does not mandate continued supply.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU supply obligation can be enforced. Market withdrawal requires 2-month advance notice. US approach is notification-based rather than obligation-based.

divergences.eu_requirement: Wholesale distribution requires specific authorisation. GDP compliance mandatory. Transaction records kept 5 years. Safety feature verification mandatory.
divergences.fda_requirement: State-level wholesale distributor licensing. No federal wholesale distribution authorisation comparable to EU system. DSCSA imposes transaction documentation requirements.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has harmonised EU-wide wholesale distribution requirements. US has fragmented state-by-state licensing with federal DSCSA overlay.

divergences.eu_requirement: Specific regulatory framework for medicine brokers (persons negotiating sale/purchase without physical handling). Must register, have permanent EU address, and comply with wholesale obligations.
divergences.fda_requirement: No specific federal regulation of medicine brokers.
divergences.compliance_implication: Any entity brokering medicines in the EU must register and comply with specific obligations. No equivalent US requirement.

divergences.eu_requirement: EU-wide mandatory common logo for legitimate online pharmacies with cryptographic verification, hyperlinks to competent authority verification system. Member States must set up verification websites.
divergences.fda_requirement: No federal common logo system for online pharmacies. BeSafeRx is an educational campaign, not a regulatory framework. State pharmacy boards regulate online pharmacies.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has a technologically integrated verification system for online medicine sales. US relies on fragmented state regulation and voluntary programs.

divergences.eu_requirement: Advertising of prescription medicinal products to the general public is PROHIBITED. Only non-prescription products may be advertised to consumers (with restrictions).
divergences.fda_requirement: DTC advertising of prescription drugs is PERMITTED, subject to fair balance, adequate provision for brief summary, and major statement requirements.
divergences.compliance_implication: This is one of the most fundamental EU-US divergences. Companies cannot run consumer-facing prescription drug advertising campaigns in the EU. All EU promotional activity for Rx products must target healthcare professionals only.

divergences.eu_requirement: No gifts, pecuniary advantages, or benefits in kind to prescribers unless inexpensive and relevant to practice. Hospitality strictly limited to main scientific purpose. Prescribers shall not solicit or accept prohibited inducements.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA does not directly regulate gifts/hospitality to prescribers. Industry self-regulation via PhRMA Code. Sunshine Act requires transparency (Open Payments) but does not prohibit payments.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU statutory prohibition is stricter than US transparency approach. Companies operating in EU must maintain strict gift/hospitality policies that exceed US requirements.

divergences.eu_requirement: Free samples only to prescribers, exceptionally, with specific conditions: limited number per year, written/signed request required, no larger than smallest presentation, marked 'free sample', accompanied by SmPC copy. No samples of psychotropic/narcotic products.
divergences.fda_requirement: Samples permitted with request by licensed practitioner. PDMA requires written request, record-keeping, and prohibition of sample sales. Fewer restrictions on quantity and presentation size.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU imposes more restrictive conditions on sampling, including annual quantity limits per product and smallest-presentation requirement.

divergences.eu_requirement: Comprehensive separate regulation for gene therapy, somatic cell therapy, and tissue engineered products. Mandatory centralised procedure. CAT committee. Hospital exemption for non-routine ATMPs. 30-year traceability.
divergences.fda_requirement: Gene therapies, cellular therapies, and tissue products regulated under biologics framework (BLA) with CBER oversight. No separate ATMP regulation. No mandatory committee. No 30-year traceability.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU ATMP regulation creates additional classification requirements (CAT opinion), mandatory centralised filing, specific traceability obligations (30 years), and hospital exemption pathway with no US equivalent.

divergences.eu_requirement: Simplified registration pathway for herbal products with 30 years of traditional use (15 years in EU). No efficacy data required. Community herbal monographs established by HMPC.
divergences.fda_requirement: No equivalent pathway. Herbal products may be marketed as dietary supplements under DSHEA (no pre-market approval) or as drugs (NDA required with efficacy data).
divergences.compliance_implication: EU provides a regulated 'middle ground' for traditional herbals — more regulated than US dietary supplements but less than full drug approval. Products qualifying in one system may not qualify in the other.

divergences.eu_requirement: Specific simplified registration procedure for homeopathic products meeting conditions (oral/external, no therapeutic claims, sufficient dilution). Full labelling requirements.
divergences.fda_requirement: Homeopathic products historically marketed under CPG 400.400 enforcement discretion. FDA proposed new framework treating most homeopathic products as unapproved new drugs. HPUS recognition.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has a clear statutory registration pathway for homeopathics. US regulatory status is currently uncertain following withdrawal of enforcement discretion guidance.

divergences.eu_requirement: Prevalence-based: not more than 5 in 10,000 persons in the Community (approximately 230,000 patients in EU population). Alternative: unlikely to generate sufficient return without incentives.
divergences.fda_requirement: Patient-count based: fewer than 200,000 persons in the US. Alternative: no reasonable expectation of cost recovery.
divergences.compliance_implication: A disease may qualify as orphan in one jurisdiction but not the other due to different population bases and threshold calculations.

divergences.topic: Paediatric Investigation Plan (PIP) — mandatory paediatric development
divergences.eu_requirement: PIP required for all new MAs, line extensions, and new indications. Must be agreed with Paediatric Committee before submission. Triggered at completion of adult pharmacokinetic studies. Waivers/deferrals available.
divergences.fda_requirement: PREA requires pediatric studies for new active ingredients, new indications, new dosage forms, new dosing regimens, and new routes of administration. Triggered at NDA/BLA submission. Waivers/deferrals available.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU PIP must be agreed BEFORE MA submission (triggered at PK studies). FDA PREA requirement arises AT or DURING NDA/BLA review. EU process starts earlier and requires more upfront planning.

divergences.eu_requirement: Biosimilar pathway (Art. 10(4)) requires appropriate pre-clinical and clinical data to demonstrate similarity. No automatic interchangeability/substitution at EU level — left to Member States.
divergences.fda_requirement: BPCIA provides abbreviated licensure pathway for biosimilars. FDA can designate biosimilars as 'interchangeable' with additional data demonstrating switching produces no diminished efficacy or safety.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU does not have a federal interchangeability designation — each Member State decides substitution rules independently. US has a pathway to interchangeability designation at federal level.

10_transparency_and_access.title: Transparency, Public Access, and Patient Rights

divergences.eu_requirement: EMA shall immediately publish assessment report (EPAR) for centrally authorised products with lay-language summary, conditions of use, reasons for opinion. Publicly accessible.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA publishes approval letters, review documents, and labelling. Full review documentation available after approval but not as a structured public assessment report with lay summary.
divergences.compliance_implication: EPAR publication means EU approval rationale is immediately and comprehensively public. Companies should prepare for EPAR disclosure as part of launch communications.

divergences.eu_requirement: If applicant withdraws MA application before opinion, applicant shall communicate reasons. Agency shall make this publicly accessible and publish assessment report (after removing confidential information).
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA does not routinely publish reasons for NDA/BLA withdrawals or publish partial assessment reports for withdrawn applications.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU withdrawal is a public event. Applicants must consider reputational impact of withdrawal from centralised procedure.

divergences.eu_requirement: Clinical study report data in MA applications shall not be considered commercially confidential once MA granted, application withdrawn, or procedure completed. EU Clinical Trials Register publicly accessible.
divergences.fda_requirement: Trade secret and confidential commercial information protected. Clinical data summaries in review documents published but full clinical study reports not routinely disclosed.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's proactive publication of clinical data means competitors and the public have access to detailed study-level data. Companies must factor this into their data strategy.

divergences.eu_requirement: Member States may make centrally-eligible products available for compassionate use. CHMP may adopt opinions on conditions. If compassionate use programme exists, applicant shall ensure patient access between authorisation and market placement.
divergences.fda_requirement: Expanded Access (compassionate use) regulated under 21 CFR Part 312 Subpart I. Individual patient, intermediate-size, and treatment IND pathways. No obligation to continue access post-approval.
divergences.fda_legal_basis: 21 CFR 312.300-320; Right to Try Act (21 USC §360bbb-0a)
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has CHMP-level opinions and Member State programmes. US has FDA-regulated expanded access. EU may impose obligation to maintain access between approval and launch.

divergences.eu_requirement: Formal three-tier variation classification: Type IA (do-and-tell, 12-month notify), Type IB (tell-wait-and-do, 30-day review), Type II (major, 60-day assessment). Extensions as separate category. Work-sharing for multi-country products.
divergences.fda_requirement: Changes reported via supplements: Prior Approval Supplement (PAS), Changes Being Effected in 30 Days (CBE-30), Changes Being Effected (CBE-0), Annual Report. No formal variation classification system equivalent to EU tiers.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU and FDA have different categorization systems for post-approval changes, with different timelines, documentation requirements, and approval mechanisms. Parallel submissions require separate change management strategies.

divergences.eu_requirement: MAH may implement urgent safety restrictions immediately with simultaneous notification. Authority has 24 hours to accept/refuse. MAH must submit variation application within 15 days.
divergences.fda_requirement: CBE-0 supplement for labelling changes to add/strengthen safety warnings, contraindications, or precautions. Can be implemented immediately upon filing.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU has 24-hour authority response window and 15-day follow-up variation. FDA CBE-0 has no authority response window — change is self-implementing.

divergences.eu_requirement: PRAC oversight. Draft protocol submitted 60 days before start. Written endorsement required before commencement. Final report within 12 months of data collection end. Substantial amendments require prior approval.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA may require PMR/PMC studies. No equivalent committee oversight of study protocols. FDA reviews study designs but process less formalised than PRAC endorsement.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU PASS has more regulatory touchpoints (protocol endorsement, amendment approval, 12-month report deadline). FDA PMR/PMC has milestones but less structured protocol review.

divergences.eu_requirement: Agency may require post-authorisation efficacy studies when understanding of disease/methodology indicates previous evaluations may need significant revision.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA may require confirmatory trials under Accelerated Approval. FDORA (2022) expanded FDA's authority to require post-market studies/trials.
divergences.compliance_implication: Both jurisdictions can require post-approval efficacy studies. EU authority is broader (not limited to accelerated approvals).

divergences.eu_requirement: Two-limb definition: (a) substance presented as treating/preventing disease; OR (b) substance administered to restore/correct/modify physiological functions by pharmacological/immunological/metabolic action, or for medical diagnosis. Borderline products default to medicinal product classification.
divergences.fda_requirement: Drug defined as articles intended for use in diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease; or articles intended to affect the structure or any function of the body. Biological product separately defined.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's presentation limb can capture products not classified as drugs in the US (e.g., certain health claims). EU borderline rule means doubtful products default to pharmaceutical regulation.

divergences.eu_requirement: EU uses 'falsified medicinal product' — false representation of identity (packaging, labelling, name, composition), source (manufacturer, country), or history (distribution records). Distinct from IP infringement and unintentional quality defects.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA uses 'counterfeit' — drug sold under another drug's name with or without consent. Federal Anti-Tampering Act. No distinction between falsification categories.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's broader 'falsified' definition captures more scenarios than 'counterfeit'. Different terminology in anti-falsification policies and reporting.

divergences.eu_requirement: Directive does not apply to: magistral formulae (pharmacy-prepared per individual prescription) or officinal formulae (pharmacy-prepared per pharmacopoeia for direct supply to patients served by that pharmacy).
divergences.fda_requirement: Pharmacy compounding regulated under §503A (traditional) and §503B (outsourcing facilities). Not exempt from FDA oversight but subject to different requirements than NDA-approved drugs.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU pharmacy exemptions are broader (complete exclusion from Directive scope). US compounding is regulated under FDA with specific conditions.

divergences.eu_requirement: Commission may impose fines up to 5% of EU turnover and periodic penalties up to 2.5% of average daily EU turnover for 22 listed obligation failures. Court of Justice has unlimited jurisdiction.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA enforcement includes warning letters, injunctions, seizures, consent decrees, and criminal prosecution. No percentage-of-revenue fine structure for regulatory non-compliance.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's revenue-based fine structure can result in very large penalties for large companies. FDA relies more on injunctive/criminal enforcement.

divergences.eu_requirement: EMA may charge fees to MAHs for pharmacovigilance activities, provided independence is strictly guaranteed. Fees set by Council regulation.
divergences.fda_requirement: PDUFA user fees cover FDA review activities. No separate pharmacovigilance fee. GDUFA covers generic review.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU annual pharmacovigilance fees are separate from MA application fees. US PDUFA fees are primarily application-based.

14_rapid_alert_and_recall.title: Rapid Alert, Recall, and Crisis Management

divergences.eu_requirement: Structured rapid alert system across all Member States. If suspected serious risk and product reached patients: urgent public announcements within 24 hours with recall order. System operates during and outside normal working hours.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA Recall system (Class I/II/III). MedWatch Safety Alerts. No statutory 24-hour announcement requirement. Recalls may be voluntary or FDA-mandated.
divergences.fda_legal_basis: 21 CFR Part 7 (recalls); 21 USC §360h (mandatory recall for devices only)
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's 24-hour public announcement requirement for patient-level recalls is more prescriptive than FDA's tiered voluntary recall system.

divergences.eu_requirement: MAH shall notify competent authority if product ceases to be placed on market (temporarily or permanently) no less than 2 months before interruption. Shall state reasons, particularly if based on safety grounds.
divergences.fda_requirement: Manufacturers must notify FDA of permanent discontinuance or interruption of certain drugs at least 6 months before (or as soon as practicable). FDA Drug Shortage provisions.
divergences.compliance_implication: US requires 6-month advance notice (for qualifying drugs); EU requires 2-month notice. Different triggering criteria and product scope.

divergences.eu_requirement: Staff of competent authority responsible for authorisations shall have no financial or other interests in pharmaceutical industry. Annual declaration of financial interests mandatory.
divergences.fda_requirement: Government ethics rules apply (18 USC §208). Financial disclosure required. FDA employees subject to post-employment restrictions. But pharmaceutical stock ownership is not always prohibited (recusal system).
divergences.compliance_implication: EU's explicit prohibition on financial interests (not just disclosure) is stricter than US recusal-based approach.

divergences.eu_requirement: All EMA committee members, rapporteurs, and experts shall make annual declarations of financial interests and specific declarations at each meeting. All indirect interests entered in a public register.
divergences.fda_requirement: FDA Advisory Committee members file financial disclosure. FDA can grant waivers for conflicts. Special Government Employee rules apply.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU public register of interests provides greater transparency. FDA waiver system allows some conflicted advisors to participate.

divergences.eu_requirement: Member States shall ensure appropriate collection systems for unused or expired medicinal products.
divergences.fda_requirement: No federal mandate for collection systems. DEA runs National Prescription Drug Take Back Day. Some state-level requirements.
divergences.compliance_implication: EU Member States must have formal collection systems. US relies on voluntary and DEA-sponsored programs.
